thesis_profile.market_mechanism: in the liquid perp-covered core, leader-continuation alpha and derivatives carry-dislocation alpha fail in different market regimes that are best discriminated by cross-sectional momentum dispersion rather than name-level realized volatility: high cross-sectional dispersion separates winners from losers and favors leader-continuation, low dispersion indicates compressed or rotational markets and favors residualized carry-dislocation
thesis_profile.directional_claim: rank liquid perp-covered names higher by blending a leader-continuation sub-signal and a carry-dislocation sub-signal where the blend weight is driven by per-timestamp cross-sectional dispersion of medium-horizon momentum: high-dispersion timestamps lean toward leader continuation, low-dispersion timestamps lean toward carry dislocation
thesis_profile.factor_formula: compute a v9-style leader-continuation sub-score and a v61-style carry-dislocation sub-score at the name level, then blend them using a smooth blend weight derived from per-timestamp cross-sectional dispersion of |momentum_20|: high cross-sectional dispersion shifts weight toward leader continuation, low dispersion shifts weight toward carry dislocation; apply common quality screens for distress, blow-up volatility, and broken-tape setups
